core_mechanism: the equilibrium/ensemble-average value of each macroscopic observable is already imprinted on every individual stationary eigenmode of the system's linear evolution operator within a narrow spectral band, so relaxation to a single universal steady state arises from dephasing among these near-degenerate eigenmodes rather than from any dynamical mixing
brief_reason: The abstract states that thermalization happens 'at the level of individual eigenstates', that time evolution plays 'a merely auxiliary role', and that any single eigenstate in the microcanonical energy window yields the same thermal average, which is exactly the mechanism of eigenmode-encoded equilibration via dephasing.
